danger_signals.visual_form: Character interaction portrait/dialogue box with unidentified man
danger_signals.meaning: Potential loss of funds; player must decide whether to trust character

danger_signals.visual_form: News broadcast with somber music playing
danger_signals.meaning: Player has been robbed; financial loss confirmed

danger_signals.visual_form: News broadcast with somber musical tone
danger_signals.meaning: Accident has occurred (falling chandelier, spill down stairs mentioned in premise)
Married character steals player funds instead of providing cash flow

danger_signals.visual_form: Lucky Slot machine appears (final chance mechanic)
danger_signals.meaning: Player has lost all money; one final gamble offered before game over

safety_signals.visual_form: Character portrait with favorable dialogue outcome
safety_signals.meaning: Character is trustworthy; player receives cash reward or future benefit

safety_signals.visual_form: News broadcast with cheery, upbeat music
safety_signals.meaning: Player's charitable choice to help struggling patron pays off later

safety_signals.visual_form: Spouse portrait displayed; increased cash flow in game state
safety_signals.meaning: Marriage partner is legitimate; provides ongoing financial benefit

safety_signals.visual_form: HAL Palace/Epic Palace environment with dealer and attendant NPCs
safety_signals.meaning: Safe, controlled gaming environment; staff facilitate play

safety_signals.visual_form: Money counter UI element showing substantial funds
safety_signals.meaning: Player is in secure financial position; can continue gambling safely

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: HAI logo (HAL America, Inc.) on slot machines and game select screen
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Casino interior setting with HAI branding; replaced Epic Palace branding from Japanese version

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Epic logo on slot machines and game select screen
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Casino interior setting with Epic branding; regional identifier for Famicom/JP release

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Character portraits representing business people, other patrons, casino employees
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Pickpockets, con artists, crooked spouses (disguised as normal patrons)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Downtown Las Vegas backdrop; airplane arrival sequence establishing setting

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Graphics sequence showing airplane arrival at fictional casino (HAL/Epic Palace)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player given initial $700 bankroll; must choose between 'Vegas Dream' (story mode) or 'One Spot' (pure gambling)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Casino game interfaces (Keno, Blackjack, Roulette, Slot Machine) with betting UI
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Random character interactions interrupt normal play; player responses affect future outcomes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Local Las Vegas news broadcast display on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cheery music plays; player receives cash or hears about reward from previous kindness

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Local Las Vegas news broadcast display on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Somber music plays; player hears about theft, injury, or loss event

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Spouse character portrait displayed; marriage confirmation in dialogue
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Spouse either provides ongoing cash flow (legitimate) or steals funds (crooked variant)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Money counter/bankroll display increases; mansion acquisition possible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can hire household staff; quality of life indicators improve in narrative

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ending sequence showing player reclining by pool with staff; limousine departure to show
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game victory achieved; narrative concludes successfully

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lucky Slot machine appears (Vegas Dream mode) or game over screen (One Spot mode)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final chance offered via slot machine; if lost, staff bid farewell and departure sequence plays

environmental_storytelling.example: Downtown Las Vegas backdrop visible throughout gameplay establishes geographic/temporal context (1988-1990 era Vegas)
HAL Palace (US) vs. Epic Palace (JP) names communicate regional publisher identity and corporate presence within game world
Character portraits and dialogue indicate social roles (business people, patrons, employees, romantic interests) without explicit labels
Las Vegas news station reports positive/negative events caused by player choices, making moral consequences visible and audible
Mansion acquisition and household staff hiring display player's rising social status within game world economy

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Initiates social interaction dialogue; appears as normal patron until theft occurs
Initiates social interaction dialogue; rewards player's kindness in future news broadcast
Provides ongoing cash flow; increases player bankroll during gameplay
Steals player funds instead of providing cash; causes financial loss
Provides ongoing cash flow; increases player bankroll during gameplay
Steals player funds instead of providing cash; causes financial loss
Offer Lucky Slot machine as final chance; if lost, bid farewell and initiate departure sequence

notes: Vegas Dream uses narrative consequence systems to communicate world-state through news broadcasts with musical tone cues (cheery vs. somber). Danger/safety signals are primarily social (character interactions with betrayal risk) rather than mechanical/combat-based. Faction indicators are corporate/regional (HAL Palace vs. Epic Palace branding) rather than adversarial. The game communicates moral alignment through character reliability tests that affect long-term gameplay (marriage outcomes, theft events). Regional differences show corporate identity substitution (HAI logo replacing Epic logo) without gameplay changes, suggesting manifestation layer focuses on corporate branding within the same casino environment.
sources_used: tcrf - Vegas Dream entry detailing regional differences, character interactions, and slot machine branding changes
wikipedia - Vegas Dream article covering gameplay mechanics, character interactions, social events, endings, and game modes
